message:
fix(mux-client,swarm,e2e): reserve-commit channel pattern, scheduler lint fixes, and mux server readiness

Vendored mux client (mux_client.rs):
- Replace bare tx.send(delta).await with reserve-then-commit pattern
  (tx.reserve().await + permit.send(delta)) in pane_delta_send() for
  both asupersync-runtime and default paths. This avoids a subtle
  race where send() could complete but the value is dropped before the
  receiver processes it under heavy channel contention. Both runtime
  variants now handle the closed-receiver case gracefully (no-op).
- Two new tests: pane_delta_send_delivers_via_reserve_commit validates
  the happy path, pane_delta_send_is_noop_when_receiver_closed validates
  graceful degradation when receiver is dropped.

Swarm scheduler (swarm_scheduler.rs):
- Prefix unused parameter with underscore (_now_ms in evaluate_readonly)
- Fix destructuring pattern in rebalance overloaded/underloaded filters
  to avoid binding reference-behind-reference (entry.1 instead of &l)
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] + doc comment to now_ms() utility

Swarm work queue (swarm_work_queue.rs):
- Add config() accessor for read-only queue configuration access
- Fix would_create_cycle test calls: use .to_string() instead of .into()
  for explicit String conversion (clippy type_complexity)

Mux server (frankenterm-mux-server):
- Add explicit "frankenterm-mux-server-ready" log line after listeners
  bind successfully, reporting unix_domains and tls_servers counts
  for deterministic readiness detection by e2e scripts

CLI (frankenterm/src/main.rs):
- Add resize field to Status command test fixture (struct field added
  upstream, test needed alignment)

E2E mux server script (scripts/e2e_mux_server.sh):
- Add binary size budget check (MAX_BINARY_BYTES, default 20 MiB) to
  catch size regressions early in CI
- Use "frankenterm-mux-server-ready" as primary readiness marker in
  start_server() with legacy patterns as fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ FT_BIN="${FT_BIN:-ft}"
 SOCKET_PATH="${SOCKET_PATH:-}"
 LOG_PATH="${LOG_PATH:-/tmp/frankenterm-mux-server.e2e.log}"
 TIMEOUT_SECS="${TIMEOUT_SECS:-20}"
+MAX_BINARY_BYTES="${MAX_BINARY_BYTES:-20971520}" # 20 MiB budget
 DRY_RUN=0
 
 if [[ "${1:-}" == "--dry-run" ]]; then
@@ -97,8 +98,8 @@ start_server() {
       return 1
     fi
     sleep 1
-    # Give server a chance to initialize before follow-up steps.
-    if grep -qiE "listening|mux-startup|local" "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
+    # Prefer explicit readiness marker; keep legacy fallback patterns for compatibility.
+    if grep -qiE "frankenterm-mux-server-ready|listening|mux-startup" "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
       return 0
     fi
   done
@@ -107,6 +108,30 @@ start_server() {
   kill -0 "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null
 }
 
+check_binary_size_budget() {
+  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    log "[DRY-RUN] binary size <= ${MAX_BINARY_BYTES} bytes"
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  local server_path=""
+  if [[ "$SERVER_BIN" == */* ]]; then
+    server_path="$SERVER_BIN"
+  else
+    server_path="$(command -v "$SERVER_BIN" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+  fi
+
+  if [[ -z "$server_path" || ! -f "$server_path" ]]; then
+    log "cannot resolve binary path for size check: $SERVER_BIN"
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  local binary_size
+  binary_size="$(wc -c <"$server_path" | tr -d '[:space:]')"
+  log "binary_size_bytes=${binary_size} budget_bytes=${MAX_BINARY_BYTES} path=${server_path}"
+  [[ "$binary_size" -le "$MAX_BINARY_BYTES" ]]
+}
+
 check_socket() {
   if [[ -z "$SOCKET_PATH" ]]; then
     log "SOCKET_PATH not set; skipping strict socket existence check"
@@ -209,6 +234,7 @@ graceful_shutdown() {
 main() {
   log "e2e_mux_server: starting"
   run_step "start frankenterm-mux-server" start_server || true
+  run_step "binary size budget" check_binary_size_budget || true
   run_step "verify socket path" check_socket || true
   run_step "ft robot state" robot_state || true
   run_step "ft robot send + search" robot_send_and_search || true
